mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces equity discount rates, compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 45% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.5% indicates severe labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising real personal income and declining industrial production creates demand-supply imbalances that trigger inflationary expectations and monetary tightening fears, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting negative returns, or positive returns dominate in more than 60% of periods when RSAFS growth outpaces INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months with declining INDPRO, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600,000 units indicates a collapse in residential investment demand that drags down GDP growth and causes negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods when HOUST is below 600, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Low inflation combined with near-zero interest rates creates a liquidity trap environment where monetary policy loses efficacy, causing capital flight from equities to fixed income and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX outperforms Treasury bills in more than 65% of months when both CPIAUCSL growth is below 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion and consumer spending, generating positive forward equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth versus 20-day returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year window, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2%, or the signal predictive power vanishes during post-recession recovery phases.
